title: Predator-Prey Population Dynamics
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Interactive MicroSim modeling Lotka-Volterra predator-prey oscillations with an animated meadow and real-time population graphs.

learningObjectives: Model predator-prey oscillations and predict how changing parameters affects population dynamics
Analyze the feedback loops that create cyclical population patterns
Predict the consequences of removing predators or introducing disease to an ecosystem
Connect simulation behavior to real-world examples such as the lynx-hare cycle
prerequisites: Basic understanding of food webs and trophic levels
Ability to interpret line graphs with multiple variables

keyQuestions: Why do predator and prey populations oscillate rather than reaching equilibrium?
What happens to prey populations when predators are removed?
How does a disease outbreak in the prey population affect predators?
What parameter changes lead to stable vs. unstable oscillations?
commonMisconceptions: Removing predators always benefits prey populations in the long term
Predator and prey populations peak at the same time rather than being offset
Predator-prey cycles always have the same amplitude and period
assessmentOpportunities: Students predict outcomes of the Disease and Remove Predators buttons before pressing them
Students explain the time lag between predator and prey population peaks
Students connect simulation results to the Yellowstone wolf reintroduction case study
